Best for: General browser automation, testing, scraping, agent workflows
Pros: Cross-browser, strong auto-waiting, great built-ins, parallelism, robust modern default
Replaceable by: Often replaces Selenium, many Puppeteer use cases, some Cypress use cases playwright+1

Pros: Huge ecosystem, many languages, long-lived standard
Cons: More boilerplate, more flakiness, slower ergonomics
Replaceable by: Often replaced by Playwright in greenfield work getautonoma+1

Best for: Chrome/Chromium automation, scraping, direct browser control
Pros: Lightweight, direct CDP control, strong Chrome-first scripting
Replaceable by: Can be replaced by Playwright in many cases, but not always 1:1 firecrawl+1

Pros: Excellent DX, easy for UI tests
Replaceable by: Not usually a full replacement for Playwright/Selenium getautonoma+1

Cons: Less compelling than Playwright for new projects
Replaceable by: Often overlaps with Selenium-style stacks eviltester
Not a replacement for general cross-language tooling github
Complements rather than replaces Playwright/Selenium firecrawl+1

Pros: Handles changing sites better than brittle scripts
Replaceable by: Complements classic tools for task automation skyvern
